title: Tangy Crunchy Vietnamese Sea Bass Canh Chua
description: An indulgent, low-carb twist on the classic sour soup featuring crispy fried sea bass, a deeply savory bonito-infused broth, and a subtle dusting of earthy matcha.

ingredients.name: matcha powder
ingredients.notes: culinary grade for a jammy, earthy finish

instructions: Pat the sea bass fillets dry with paper towels and season them with salt.
Dredge the fish fillets in the crushed pork rinds, pressing firmly to adhere.
Heat the coconut oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat and fry the fish until golden and crunchy, about 4 minutes per side.
Bring four cups of water to a simmer in a separate pot and steep the bonito flakes for 5 minutes before straining them out.
Whisk the dissolved tamarind paste, fish sauce, and allulose into the hot bonito broth to create a tangy, savory base.
Divide the hot broth into serving bowls and stir in a tiny pinch of matcha powder into each bowl for a jammy, complex undertone.
Gently place the crispy fried sea bass fillets on top of the broth to keep the crust crunchy.
Garnish generously with the chopped dill, scallions, and a final light dusting of matcha before serving.
